title: [Windows 10] `podman machine init` failing on several points
body:
### Issue Description

After running a fresh install of podman on a work machine, I can't complete `podman machine init`. I've tried several additional arguments including `--disk-size 25` (the C:\ drive does not have 100GiB available) and `--rootful`. Consistently getting the same errors even in elevated prompts.

```sh
# roughly translates to "There's been a timeout of the process because no response was
# received from the virtual machine or container" and I think it's an OS/WSL2 error?
Er is een time-out van de bewerking opgetreden omdat er geen reactie is ontvangen van de virtuele machine of container.
# Error :
Foutcode: Wsl/Service/CreateInstance/HCS_E_CONNECTION_TIMEOUT
# I think Podman is spitting this out, and it might be causing the above?
Error: package permissions restore of shadow-utils on guest OS failed: command C:\Users\Martin\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps\wsl.exe [-u root -d podman-true rpm --restore shadow-utils] failed: exit status 0xffffffff
```

```ps
PS C:\Users\Riven\> wsl --version
WSL-versie: 2.4.13.0
Kernelversie: 5.15.167.4-1
WSLg-versie: 1.0.65
MSRDC-versie: 1.2.5716
Direct3D-versie: 1.611.1-81528511
DXCore-versie: 10.0.26100.1-240331-1435.ge-release
Windows-versie: 10.0.19045.5608
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Get yourself a fully update Windows 10 pro
2. Install podman from the latest GH release
3. `podman machine init`


### Describe the results you received

See the issue section

### Describe the results you expected

success as per [the guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/podman-for-windows.md#machine-init-process)

### podman info output

```yaml
AMD64 running podman 5.4.2, installed fresh this morning.
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Domain-managed accounts, locally installed software stack, Windows 10. If it's trying to use a synced/mirrored directory that might be difficult, but those should only be `C:\Users\Riven\{Desktop,Documents}`
Shouldn't be anything else of note, I tried with a minimal `$ENV:PATH` to rule out issues there.

### Additional information

-

comments.body: What happens if you try to install a wsl ubuntu machine, ie `wsl --install`?
Oh that works fine, I have several WSL instances up and running on this machine. Note the WSL install isn't new, it's _specifically_ podman that's new

```
Error: package permissions restore of shadow-utils on guest OS failed: command C:\Users\Martin\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps\wsl.exe [-u root -d podman-true rpm --restore shadow-utils] failed: exit status 0xffffffff
```

Are you getting this each time your init a podman machine (i.e. machine rm, machine init) ?
@l0rd i dont see why this should fail on Windows 10 unless the `WSLInvoke` function is invalid on Windows 10?
I get that on every attempt, yes. I didn't open an issue until about 6 tries in

It would be helpful to have the full output of the command `podman machine init --log-level=debug`. Also the Windows 10 details may be useful ([build number](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/troubleshooting#check-your-build-number)) to reproduce the problem. Also the output of `wsl -l -v` to see what version of WSL is used by other distributions.

+1 to the comment from @l0rd , could you also please try running `wsl --update` to see if it fixes it? If it doesn't could you then show the output of `wsl --version` to make sure you're on the latest?
I'll rerun at the office tomorrow, I always make sure my WSL is up-to-date as it makes half my job a much more pleasant environment to do so. The other half being people

Sorry, yesterday was full of unexpected surprises.

> If it doesn't could you then show the output of wsl --version to make sure you're on the latest?

@craigloewen-msft it's unchanged from the version listed when originally opening the issue, but just to be sure:
```cmd
❯ wsl --update && wsl --version
REM Checking for updates
Controleren op updates.
REM The most recent version of WSL is already installed
De meest recente versie van Windows-subsysteem voor Linux is al geïnstalleerd.
WSL-versie: 2.4.13.0
Kernelversie: 5.15.167.4-1
WSLg-versie: 1.0.65
MSRDC-versie: 1.2.5716
Direct3D-versie: 1.611.1-81528511
DXCore-versie: 10.0.26100.1-240331-1435.ge-release
Windows-versie: 10.0.19045.5737
```
@l0rd all running distros are happily chugging away on wsl2
```cmd
❯ wsl -l -v
  NAME           STATE           VERSION
* AlmaLinux-9    Running         2
  Artix          Running         2
  chimera        Running         2

REM log will be attached here
❯ podman machine init --log-level=debug --disk-size 25 2> D:\podman-stderr.log
Looking up Podman Machine image at quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl:5.4 to create VM
8f46e8ebcbc2b302db7edb334fb9473ab525f6d6ed96696a6f54b4a91df75bd6
Extracting compressed file: podman-machine-default-amd64: done
Importing operating system into WSL (this may take a few minutes on a new WSL install)...
REM successfully registered the machine
De bewerking is voltooid
REM same as before, the podman error about shadow-utils is at the end of the log file
Er is een time-out van de bewerking opgetreden omdat er geen reactie is ontvangen van de virtuele machine of container.
Foutcode: Wsl/Service/CreateInstance/HCS_E_CONNECTION_TIMEOUT
```

[podman-stderr.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19777116/podman-stderr.log)

---
edit: I didn't realize I could have podman clean up before, but whatever is causing the init failure is also making sure podman can't remove the leftover VM.
```cmd
❯ podman machine rm --log-level=debug
time="2025-04-16T14:37:28+02:00" level=info msg="podman filtering at log level debug"
time="2025-04-16T14:37:28+02:00" level=debug msg="Using Podman machine with `wsl` virtualization provider"
time="2025-04-16T14:37:28+02:00" level=debug msg="socket length for C:\\Users\\Martin\\.config\\containers\\podman\\machine\\wsl is 53"
time="2025-04-16T14:37:28+02:00" level=debug msg="socket length for C:\\Users\\Martin\\.local\\share\\containers\\podman\\machine\\wsl is 58"
time="2025-04-16T14:37:28+02:00" level=debug msg="socket length for C:\\Users\\Martin\\.local\\share\\containers\\podman\\machine\\wsl\\cache is 64"
time="2025-04-16T14:37:28+02:00" level=debug msg="socket length for C:\\Users\\Martin\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\podman is 41"
time="2025-04-16T14:37:28+02:00" level=debug msg="socket length for C:\\Users\\Martin\\.config\\containers\\podman\\machine\\wsl\\podman-machine-default.json is 81"
Error: podman-machine-default: VM does not exist
time="2025-04-16T14:37:28+02:00" level=debug msg="Shutting down engines"
```

Just to clarify, I already had uninstalled podman. So this is a fresh install, from a freshly downloaded release @ latest (which is the same version as I opened the issue with)

And before I forget: w10 pro 22H2 build 19045.5737.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fe8572bc-53b3-4c85-9010-63fd4c874de4)

Could you please try taking WSL logs and attach them to this thread?

Instructions are here: 
https://aka.ms/wsllogs

Must be an Easter miracle or something, as I was rerunning it to collect logs it actually went through this time. I have no idea what changed. I did not check for podman updates and haven't yet rebooted the machine for any Windows updates that may be ready now, and yet it seems to work

Never mind, it was a lucky run. Have some logs!
There's some noise at the start because of a failed run in a retry leaving me with an existing VM that podman both claims doesn't exist (it failed initializing it) and can't create because it's registered in WSL.

[WslLogs-2025-04-22_09-28-57.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19845422/WslLogs-2025-04-22_09-28-57.zip)
This could be a WSL issue, I've created a thread for us to investigate it on the WSL team here: https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/12862

Hey [Podman Desktop](https://github.com/podman-desktop/podman-desktop) contributors here 👋 We provide a method for our users to init a Podman Machine on windows through our GUI. Following some recent complain about errors in this process we investigated a bit through our telemetry data. When an error occurred during this process we collect the `stderr` of the `podman machine init` command executed among other information about the user system. To isolate this specific error, I filtered all init failure to include the `package permissions restore of shadow-utils on guest OS failed` string in the `stderr`. The data showed bellow goes from January 1 2024 to today. ### **Error hit per months** We average at `46.5` hit per months on this specific error. ### **Error hits per months grouped by WSL Version** ℹ️ No clear correlation ### **Error hits per months grouped by WSL Kernel Version** ℹ️ No clear correlation ### **Error hits per months grouped by OS Version** ℹ️ No clear correlation ### **Error hits per months grouped by Podman CLI Version** ℹ️ No clear correlation --- From what I can see in the telemetry, it seems that some users are retrying several time, sometime it will works, sometime they try a few time and fails, but the next day it seems to be working. Pretty hard to understand, but a machine reboot might help.
